data.title: Microservices
data.description: Netflix utilizes a microservices architecture to handle its vast operations, allowing independent deployment and scaling of different services.
data.technical_spec: Deploy hundreds of independent microservices, each handling a distinct aspect of functionality.

data.title: Cloud-based Infrastructure
data.description: The service runs on AWS, leveraging cloud computing for scalability and redundancy.
data.technical_spec: Amazon Web Services support for dynamic scaling and high availability.

data.title: CDN Integration
data.description: Netflix employs a content delivery network (CDN) to distribute video content efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Use of Open Connect to cache content closer to users for reduced latency.

data.title: Event-Driven Architecture
data.description: This approach allows Netflix to react to events in real time, improving responsiveness.
data.technical_spec: Implementation of Kafka for managing streaming data and message queues.

data.title: Resilience Patterns
data.description: Netflix designs systems with high fault tolerance to maintain service amidst failures.
data.technical_spec: Hystrix and other circuits for real-time fault tolerance and isolation.
